Project Overview

What this project does

Classifies network connections into 5 categories: Normal, DoS, Probe, R2L, U2R using a tuned Random Forest on the NSL-KDD benchmark dataset.

Pipeline steps

  • Load and map NSL-KDD into 5 main attack classes
  • EDA — class distribution, protocol stats, feature distributions
  • Preprocessing — one-hot encoding, StandardScaler, column alignment
  • Feature engineering — 6 new features added (total: 128)
  • Train 4 models: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest (default + tuned)
  • Hyperparameter tuning via GridSearchCV with 3-fold cross-validation
  • Evaluation: confusion matrix, ROC curves, classification report, learning curve
  • Feature importance + SHAP explainability
